item.title: A reluctant Intertek risks testing investors’ patience
item.description: FTSE 100 company has rejected previous EQT takeover offers saying they undervalued it

item.title: The Iran war dilemma for central bankers
item.description: What happened after the pandemic is an imperfect guide for the latest shock

item.title: How architecture can save lives
item.description: Carefully designed homes in Tanzania have been shown to lower disease rates among children

item.title: Why Americans dread AI
item.description: Silicon Valley encourages the view that the technology is unstoppable — and Trump seems to agree

item.title: Chipmaker Cerebras joins OpenAI’s inner circle — for a price
item.description: Launching into the magic of the Altman-osphere could prove to be quite a windfall

item.title: Labour’s turmoil is now uncontrollable
item.description: Keir Starmer may take responsibility, but he has sidestepped accountability

item.title: The Trump Trade 2.0
item.description: Plus, the ‘buy Europe’ trade

item.title: Will AI turn us all into hipsters and artisans?
item.description: There is good reason to be dubious about the notion that automation will supplant all demand for human labour

item.title: Why personalised pricing could be a good deal for shoppers
item.description: Selling the same thing to different people at a different price sounds unfair, but it needn’t be bad news for consumers

item.title: The UK strategy for the City needs more ambition as market share slips
item.description: A hyperactive reform programme is not enough to drive the urgent national mission of driving growth and investment

item.title: We are living in the age of asymmetry
item.description: Power flows less from size or wealth than from the ability to convert imbalance into leverage

item.title: Why DS Smith’s global cardboard box merger fell apart
item.description: International Paper is quickly reversing a transatlantic takeover in a century-old industry

item.title: BYD and peers make strides in every market but their own
item.description: Beijing is switching its attention to newer sectors such as AI and robotics

item.title: Polarisation and partisan redistricting in the US
item.description: Supreme Court ruling has deepened an already ugly battle over gerrymandering

item.title: What Victory Day means to Russia now
item.description: A commemoration of those who died in the second world war is being used to lend legitimacy to war in Ukraine

item.title: GameStop’s bid for eBay is wild — and it might just work
item.description: Shareholders in the ecommerce company are getting a good deal, at least on paper, with an offer of $28bn of cash

item.title: Fomo is a poor motivation for EU tech policy
item.description: Europe’s large public sectors are a powerful tool to build a market for native tech

item.title: A weakened Trump arrives at Xi’s court
item.description: China holds the cards — and might settle for flashy but empty announcements while playing a long game

item.title: There was nothing here to save Starmer
item.description: Voters need hope, but so do his MPs — and he did not give them any

item.title: US jobs: two in a row
item.description: Plus, how to protect central bank independence

item.title: American agriculture is broken
item.description: Fixing it will require much more than the new farm bill can deliver

item.title: Will the ECB shift tack on the digital euro?
item.description: Central bank appears to be shifting its focus more to wholesale markets

item.title: We need to protect Britain against the tyranny of the minority
item.description: Our first-past-the-post electoral system cannot handle the new era of multi-party politics

item.title: How the UK can use the bond markets’ ‘kindness of strangers’
item.description: There is a way to reduce the risk of a run on gilts and decrease the cost of government borrowing

item.title: We cannot have the AI model advising clients. They might think they don’t need us
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist
